name: TRF2 overexpression caused premature skin deterioration and increased cancer in mice
summary: TRF2 gain-of-function biology is a safety and calibration boundary for simple shelterin-upregulation strategies.
statement: Mice overexpressing TRF2 in skin developed telomere loss, increased DNA damage, premature skin deterioration, hyperpigmentation, and increased skin cancer.
quantitative_note: The source reports hypersensitivity of K5TRF2 keratinocytes to ultraviolet irradiation and DNA crosslinking agents.
caveats: This is a transgenic overexpression hazard model rather than a therapeutic dosing study.
It argues against assuming that more shelterin activity is automatically protective.
